body.title: [sprint] Full pytest suite not hermetic — ~282 tests need live bus/Postgres/inference
body.description:
Running the full `tests/` suite with the rack DOWN yields 144 failed + 138 errors against 3597 passed. Root cause is non-hermetic tests, NOT code regressions: ~90 need the IMAP bus up, ~45 need Postgres (psycopg/UU_HOME_DB_URL), ~14 need the inference proxy/Ollama. `tests/igor/*` (28) also error at collection without UU_HOME_DB_URL. None of the substrate's pure-logic tests fail. This defeats the day-close audit's 'tests are the gate' step: the suite can't go green without standing the whole rack up first.

Proposed shape: make infra-dependent tests skip-or-mock when their dependency is absent (pytest markers + an autouse fixture that skips on missing bus/PG/inference), continuing the hermeticity migration already begun (commit 9192ef7b moved test_queue_gate onto the isolated FS store). Goal: `pytest tests/` green on a bare checkout with no rack.

**Affected files:** tests/conftest.py (skip fixtures + markers), tests/igor/* (DB-gated), bus/Postgres/inference-touching test modules, pytest.ini/pyproject markers
**Design rules:** no-sqlite (use FS-store or skip, never SQLite); test-plan-or-why-not
**Scope boundary:** IN — mark/skip/mock infra-dependent tests so a bare-checkout run is green; OUT — rewriting the devices under test, standing up CI infra
**Test plan:** `pytest tests/` on a checkout with no rack running exits 0 (infra tests skipped with reasons, not failed); a separate marked run with the rack up still exercises them
